slides.title: Amazing Arts
bullets: Prepare to read, discuss and write
slides.narration: Welcome to Grade 6 English, Unit 9: Amazing Arts. Welcome to Unit 9: Amazing Arts — the final unit of Year 6! In this unit you will explore how art, music, and creative expression can transform communities and inspire change. You will read about children who turn broken objects into beautiful instruments, empty walls into colourful murals, and dusty valleys into thriving green spaces. Through these stories and activities, you will sharpen your English skills and discover the power of creativity. In this short lecture, preview the key ideas and prepare for the independent lesson.

slides.title: What you will achieve
bullets: Read and understand a creative-arts passage and discuss its ideas in English
Use indefinite pronouns (someone, anyone, everyone, nothing, etc.) correctly in sentences
Order multiple adjectives properly before a noun (opinion → size → age → colour → origin → material)
slides.narration: By the end of this unit, you will be able to read and understand a creative-arts passage and discuss its ideas in english. you will also use indefinite pronouns (someone, anyone, everyone, nothing, etc.) correctly in sentences. you will also order multiple adjectives properly before a noun (opinion → size → age → colour → origin → material). Keep these goals in mind as you work.

slides.title: Language for precise thinking
slides.narration: Your vocabulary work begins with these words: deforestation, landscape, altitude, subtropics, peppermint, turbine. Listen to each pronunciation in the vocabulary lab, study its meaning in context, and use the word accurately in your own discussion and writing.

slides.title: Read critically
slides.narration: The reading sequence includes Amazing Arts: source text 1 and Amazing Arts: source text 2. Read actively. Identify each text's central ideas, notice viewpoint and reliability, and support every inference with relevant evidence from the text.

slides.title: Indefinite Pronouns — The Basics
bullets: What it means and why it matters Indefinite pronouns are words that replace nouns without saying exactly who or what they refer to. They let us talk about...
slides.narration: The first language focus is Indefinite Pronouns — The Basics. What it means and why it matters Indefinite pronouns are words that replace nouns without saying exactly who or what they refer to. They let us talk about people, things, or places in a general way. For example, instead of naming a specific person, you can say someone. Instead of listing every object, you can say everything. This is very useful in both speaking and writing. Notice the structure in the model examples, then apply it deliberately in connected sentences and paragraphs.

slides.title: Use English for a real purpose
bullets: Explain, support and improve your ideas
slides.narration: In speaking, you will complete Speaking 1: Vocabulary Expert Interview. Organise your ideas, use evidence and respond clearly to questions. In writing, you will plan, draft, check and revise. Use the success criteria before submitting your work.

slides.title: You are ready to begin
slides.narration: You are ready to begin. Start with the vocabulary lab, continue through reading and comprehension, apply the grammar focus, and complete the speaking and writing practices. Use AI English for hints and feedback, and bring your questions to the live sessions.
